content: Starting the engine. Constraint that drives everything: change gameplay code, see the result in under one second, never lose game state. If a decision fights that, the decision loses.
wgpu sprite batcher working: one draw call per texture atlas, instanced quads, 10k sprites at 144fps on my mid-range laptop GPU. Plenty of headroom for a 2D engine.

Hot-reload attempt #1 failed hard. Swapping the game dylib worked until any struct in the game state changed layout — then instant UB and a crash with no backtrace. Learned more about ABI stability this week than in the last two years.

problem_solutions.problem: Reloading the game dylib after a struct layout change reinterpreted old memory with the new layout — undefined behavior, random crashes.
problem_solutions.solution: Serialize world state to a versioned byte format before unload, deserialize after reload. Layout changes now migrate through serde instead of aliasing raw memory.

content: Fixed the frame-pacing stutter: I was polling winit events with the wrong control flow and occasionally presenting two frames inside one vsync interval. ControlFlow::Poll plus a fixed-timestep accumulator smoothed it out completely.

problem_solutions.problem: Frames occasionally presented twice inside one vsync interval, causing a visible stutter every few seconds.
problem_solutions.solution: Switched winit to ControlFlow::Poll and decoupled simulation from rendering with a fixed-timestep accumulator.

content: Milestone: hot-reload survives struct layout changes, keeps entity state, and round-trips in about 300ms. Recorded a clip of me retuning jump physics live while the ball keeps bouncing.
